title: Data inconsistency after adding index in another timezone
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```sql
SET time_zone = '-06:00';
create table t (`src` varchar(48),`t` timestamp,`timezone` varchar(100));
insert into t values('2000-07-29 23:15:30 -0600','2000-07-29 23:15:30 -0600','-6:00');
alter table t add index idx(t);
admin check table t;
```

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

No error.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
ERROR 8223 (HY000): data inconsistency in table: t, index: idx, handle: 1, index-values:"handle: 1, values: [KindMysqlTime 2000-07-30 19:15:30]" != record-values:"handle: 1, values: [KindMysqlTime 2000-07-30 13:15:30]"
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
c34f6fc
poc: create table t (`src` varchar(48),`t` timestamp,`timezone` varchar(100));
alter table t add index idx(t);
key_bug_features: Adding index in a different timezone causes data inconsistency
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIMESTAMP, description VARCHAR(255), nullable_col INT NULL);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, event_time, description, nullable_col) VALUES (1, '2000-07-29 23:15:30 -0600', 'Event 1', NULL);
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD INDEX idx_event_time(event_time);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE event_time IS NOT NULL AND nullable_col IS NULL;
